title: hieracium bourgaei subsp. catolanum
intro: Hieracium bourgaei subsp. catolanum, also known as Cat's Ear or Hieracium catlanum, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
